MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
Python

Magewell provides GUI Python example of AVCapturePY in dir: SDKv3\Examples\Python\.

Overview

Similar to C Sharp Examples and VB Examples, the ctypes module calls functions of SDKv3 by loading .dll files and declaring function prototype and data structures to make Python work.

from ctypes import *
...
class mw_video_signal_status(Structure):
_pack_ = 1
_fields_ = [('state',c_int32),
('x',c_int32),
('y',c_int32),
('cx',c_int32),
('cy',c_int32),
('cxTotal',c_int32),
('cyTotal',c_int32),
('bInterlaced',c_bool),
('dwFrameDuration',c_int32),
('nAspectX',c_int32),
('nAspectY',c_int32),
('bSegmentedFrame',c_bool),
('frameType',c_int32),
('colorFormat',c_int32),
('quantRange',c_int32),
('satRange',c_int32)]
...
self.m_lib_mw_capture.MWGetVideoSignalStatus.argtypes = [
c_void_p,
POINTER(mw_video_signal_status)]
...

SDKv3 uses Qt5 as the UI framework for Python.

AVCapturePY

AVCapturePY captures and renders video with interfaces of Pro Capture Family Functions and Eco Capture Family Functions. It has the same functions and program logic with AVCapture C++.

AVCapturePY

Python Submodules

Import submodules of PyQt5, PyOpenGL, numpy and pywin32 before running AVCapturePY. To install the submodules, perform the following commands.

python -m pip install PyQt5
python -m pip install PyOpenGL
python -m pip install numpy
python -m pip install pywin32

AVCapturePY encapsulates ffmpeg in mw_av_encoder to record .mp4 files instead of using PyAV.

Operation

Python will execute the code in avcapture.py as this file is an entry point.

python avcapture.py

Compile

PyInstaller is used to convert Python programs into stand-alone executables.

# Install pyinstaller
python -m pip install pyinstaller
# Converts AVCapturePY to an EXE file
# Displays the command line window
# The output folder is dist.
pyinstaller --add-binary 'mwcapture/bin;mwcapture/bin' avcapture.py
# Converts AVCapturePY to an EXE file
# Displays the command line window
# The output folder is ../../bin
pyinstaller --add-binary 'mwcapture/bin;mwcapture/bin' avcapture.py --distpath='../../bin'
# Converts AVCapturePY to an EXE file
# Hides the command line window
# The output folder is dist.
pyinstaller --add-binary 'mwcapture/bin;mwcapture/bin' -w avcapture.py
# Converts AVCapturePY to an EXE file
# Hides the command line window
# The output folder is ../../bin
pyinstaller --add-binary 'mwcapture/bin;mwcapture/bin' -w avcapture.py --distpath='../../bin'
